source: Combined from 6 source files. authored: first-principles-constraint, reversibility-doors, second-order-consequence, cynefin-classify; external: debias-authored, glm-distractor
leakage:
First-principles-constraint, reversibility-doors, and second-order-consequence are locally authored datasets — zero external leakage. Debias-authored items are locally authored cognitive bias scenarios with anchoring bias types. Some items may resemble classic cognitive bias examples from psychology literature which models may have encountered. GLM distractor items may have training data overlap. Overall leakage risk is low because locally authored items dominate the target set.

Source breakdown: 1 external dataset(s) with potential training-data leakage; 4 authored dataset(s) with zero external leakage;

label.definition: Binary decision quality on conceptual reframing tasks: whether the model correctly identifies whether a constraint is fundamental (true) or merely conventional (false); whether a decision is reversible (true) or not (false); whether a proposed action has important second-order consequences (true) or not (false).

calibration.baseline_note: Estimated from label distribution (49% true). Balanced distribution suggests items are in the target difficulty band. Actual baseline must be measured by calibration runner.
calibration.instructions: Run EVAL_RUN=calib node evals/run-calibration.js evals/datasets/calibrated/<family>-candidate-pool.jsonl to populate real calibration stats.

item_class_definitions.target: Items that directly measure the family value surface — in-domain, skill-relevant items where the skill is expected to help.
item_class_definitions.near_miss: Items that are similar to the family domain but not an exact match. Useful for calibration but may not benefit from the skill.
item_class_definitions.distractor: Items that are clearly off-target (target=false). Used to measure false-positive rate and specificity.
item_class_definitions.ambiguous: Items whose relevance to the family is unclear. Excluded from decisive splits.
